threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it the Tube instead of the Overground. Bonus points if you’ve yet to learn that no one says 'the' Dalston.
Last week on the 35, sat next to a guy singing old reggae tracks loudly. He claimed it was a tribute to the 'real Brixton'—nobody argued.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I heard a toddler shouting 'Ich will kein Gemüse!' from a stroller while their hipster parent scrolled on their phone at the market.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a dog park with a mini beer garden right next to it. Watch the pups while sipping a local brew—pure Berlin vibe.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only a local knows to avoid the BART escalator at Civic Center after 5 PM; it freezes like a block of ice. Once, I slipped and nearly went flying.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Waiting at Civic Center, a group of tourists asked a Muni driver for directions. He smiled and pointed them right back to the same bus they missed.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about the fancy bars, but they miss the real vibe in its quiet corners where locals sip café amb llet on stoops.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot a recent arrival by their confused faces while ordering a coffee; they still ask for milk in a cortado.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look confused when the bus driver shouts 'Last stop!' and everyone suddenly vanishes at a random junction.
Last night on the 196, I saw a guy quietly playing an entire saxophone solo. No one batted an eyelid, just the usual 2am vibe. Classic Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a dad on the playground teaching his kid to swear in Turkish while the mom was on her phone, blending cultures in real-time.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you find a bar that serves cocktails in vintage flowerpots. It’s the perfect blend of quirky and cozy, just like our Kiez.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by how they roll their eyes at tourists taking pics outside La Taqueria while ignoring the line outside El Farolito.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 21 late last night, and a guy serenaded us with a ukulele. For a moment, the whole bus felt like a scene from a movie – very SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think they can just hop on any tram and see everything; they miss the charm of getting lost in the narrow streets where locals actually live.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by their confused faces at the price of a café amb llet – still thinking it should be under 1 euro like at home.
